titleStmt.title: SII 9.236: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 236. (A.R. No. 393 of 1920.) ON A STONE SET UP IN THE VILLAGE DONEKALLU, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 65, Durmati, Jyēshṭha, śu. 10, Ādityavāra, corresponding probably to A.D. 1141 May 17, Saturday, not Sunday. It refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Tribhuvanamalla-Permāḍidēva ruling from his capital Kalyāṇa. Vijayāditya-Gāvuṇḍa, son of Ayyapa-Gāvuṇḍa, is stated to have set up the god Vīravijayēśvara and made a grant of 12 mattars of land, a house, a flower garden and an oil-mill for the service of the god. The record is damaged. The king who was ruling in this year was Jagadēkamalla II. It is not known whether he had the surname Tribhuvanamalla.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . samastabhuvanāśraya śrīprithvīvallabha ma
hārājādhirājaṁ paramēśvaraṁ paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśraya
kuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmattribhuvanamallapermāḍidēva
ru kalyāṇapurada nelevīḍinalu sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁge
yyuttamiralu caḷukyavikramavarṣada 65 neya durmmatisaṁvatsara
da jēṣṭasuddha dasami Ādityavāradaṁdu Ayyapagāpuṁḍana putraṁ
vijayādityagāvuṁḍaṁ pratiṣṭhegeyisida vīravijayēsvaradēvarge snānabhō
gakke jīrṇnōddhārakkaṁ panneraḍu mattaru maneya nivēśanamaṁ Oṁdu
gāṇavuṁ Oṁdu tōṁṭavuṁ sarvvēsvaraṁge dhārā
pūrvvakaṁ māḍi koṭṭaru paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ śaṣṭirvvarśa
sahaśrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ [jāyatē krimiḥ .]
Digital edition of SII 9.236 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
